attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway rider who attacks early on sprint stages or minor climbs to harvest secondary classification points (KOM) or media exposure rather than stage wins. Targets low-risk, high-visibility moves when stronger teams control the race.
team_tactics: Team shows limited commitment to defending breakaway positions, prioritizing jersey visibility and media time over sustained efforts. Cannondale allows individual opportunism but won't sacrifice sprint support or domestique strength to protect early breaks.
terrain_preference: Minor categorized climbs on otherwise flat stages where sprinter teams will control the finale. Exposed on legitimate climbing stages where pure climbers compete, though showed unexpected resilience in chase groups at Tour de France.
vulnerability: Lacks team support to convert breakaway positions into results. Abandons efforts when break is clearly doomed rather than persisting. Cannot compete for KOM points on high-mountain stages where specialists focus.
key_patterns: Targets KOM points on minor climbs when sprinter teams control pace, observed 2017 Tour
Pursues virtual race leader positions from distant GC to gain early jerseys, seen Dauphine
Abandons breakaway efforts once futility clear, efficient energy management for stage races
Team prioritizes media exposure over committing resources to defend breakaway positions
form_trajectory: neutral: 2018 showed continuation of efficient breakaway tactics but no evolution toward winning from breaks or improving team support
